{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

:::{#exr-ci-diff-means}

Given
a maximum likelihood estimate $\hvb$
and
a corresponding estimated covariance matrix
$\hS \eqdef \widehat{\Covt}(\hvb)$,
calculate a 95% confidence interval for the difference in means
comparing covariate patterns $\vx$ and $\vxs$:

$$\Delta \mu \eqdef \mu(\vx) - \mu(\vxs)$$

:::

---

:::{#sol-ci-diff-means}

::: notes

We can construct the confidence interval directly:

:::

$$\Delta \mu \in \widehat{\Delta \mu} \pm t_{n-p}(0.975) \cdot \HSE{\widehat{\Delta \mu}}$$

:::

---

:::{#exr-diff-means-as-lincom}

Express the difference in means
$\Delta \mu \eqdef \mu(\vx) - \mu(\vxs)$ as a linear function of $\vb$.

:::

---

:::{#sol-diff-means-as-lincom}

$$
\ba
\Delta \mu
&= \mu(\vx) - \mu(\vxs)
\\
&= \iprodf{\vx}{\vb} - \iprodf{(\vxs)}{\vb}
\\
&= \iprodf{\vx - \vxs}{\vb}
\\
&= \iprodf{\diffvx}{\vb}
\ea
$${#eq-diff-means-lincom}

where $\diffvx \eqdef \vx - \vxs$.

:::

---

:::{#exr-se-diff-means}

::: notes

How can we estimate the standard error of
$\widehat{\Delta \mu}$?

:::

$$\blue{\HSE{\widehat{\Delta \mu}}} = \blue{?}$$

:::

---

:::{#sol-se-diff-means}

$$
\green{\SE{\widehat{\Delta \mu}}} =
\sqrt{\red{\Var{\widehat{\Delta \mu}}}}
$${#eq-se-sqrt-var-diff-means}

By @sol-diff-means-as-lincom and [the variance of a linear combination](probability.qmd#thm-var-lincom):

$$
\ba
\red{\Var{\widehat{\Delta \mu}}}
&= \Var{\iprodf{\diffvx}{\hvb}}
\\
&= \pt{\diffvx}\Cov{\hvb}(\diffvx)
\\
&= \red{\pt{\diffvx}\Sigma(\diffvx)}
\ea
$${#eq-var-diff-means}

where $\Sigma \eqdef \Covt(\hvb)$.

---

::: notes

Expanding @eq-var-diff-means out of matrix-vector notation, we have:

:::

$$
\ba
\red{\pt{\diffvx}\Sigma(\diffvx)}
&= \sumip\sumjp (\diffvx)_i \Sigma_{ij} (\diffvx)_j
\\
&= \sumip\sumjp (\Delta x_i) \Sigma_{ij} (\Delta x_j)
\\
&= \red{\sumip\sumjp (x_i - x^*_i) \Covt(\hvb_i,\hvb_j) (x_j - x^*_j)}
\ea
$$

---

Combining @eq-var-diff-means and the Gauss-Markov theorem:

:::{#thm-est-se-diff-means}

#### Estimated variance and standard error of difference in means

$$
\orange{\hVar{\widehat{\Delta \mu}}} = \orange{\tp{\diffvx}\hat{\Sigma}(\diffvx)}
$${#eq-est-var-diff-means}

$$
\blue{\HSE{\widehat{\Delta \mu}}} =
\blue{\sqrt{\tp{\diffvx}\hat{\Sigma}(\diffvx)}}
$${#eq-est-se-diff-means}

:::

::: notes

Note: on the RHS, we have plugged in $\hat{\Sigma}$,
our estimate of $\Sigma$.

Compare this result with the formula for
[inference for predicted means](#exr-se-mean):
the only change is to replace $\vx$ with $\diffvx = \vx - \vxs$.

:::

:::

---

In R, we can compute the CI for the difference in means
by computing the predicted means for both covariate patterns
and applying the variance formula directly:

```{r}
#| label: tbl-bw-diff-means
#| tbl-cap: "95% CI for difference in birthweight means (male vs. female)"
library(dplyr)
new_data_pair <- tibble(
  sex = factor(c("female", "male"), levels = levels(bw$sex)),
  age = c(40, 40)
)

pred_pair <-
  bw_lm2 |>
  predict(
    newdata = new_data_pair,
    se.fit = TRUE
  )

design_mat <- model.matrix(delete.response(terms(bw_lm2)), new_data_pair)
delta_x <- design_mat[2, ] - design_mat[1, ]

sigma_hat <- vcov(bw_lm2)

diff_mean_hat <- diff(pred_pair$fit)
se_diff <- sqrt(t(delta_x) %*% sigma_hat %*% delta_x)
t_crit <- qt(0.975, df = bw_lm2$df.residual)

tibble(
  diff_mean = diff_mean_hat,
  se = as.numeric(se_diff),
  ci_lower = diff_mean_hat - t_crit * se,
  ci_upper = diff_mean_hat + t_crit * se
) |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)
```
